author: John Kanell
description: Hush Puppies are a Southern classic-crispy on the outside, fluffy on the inside, and perfect for fish fries or family barbecues. These are easy to make with simple pantry ingredients and fry up beautifully golden.
ingredients: Peanut or vegetable oil, for frying
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, baking soda, and black pepper.

In a separate bowl, whisk together the buttermilk and egg until well combined. Add the wet mixture and grated onion to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Let the batter rest for 10 minutes.
Heat about 2 inches of oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot to 350°F. Line a baking sheet or plate with paper towels.
Using two spoons, drop tablespoon-sized portions of batter into the hot oil, working in batches to avoid overcrowding.
Fry until golden brown, turning halfway through, about 3-4 minutes total.
Remove hush puppies with a slotted spoon or spider strainer and drain on paper towels. Serve hot.
notes: Do not overmix the batter or the hush puppies will be dense instead of fluffy.
Letting the batter rest allows the cornmeal to absorb moisture and thicken properly.
Use a thermometer to maintain oil temperature at 350°F for best results.
Fry in small batches so the oil temperature does not drop too low.
title: Hush Puppies
